title: Persist transport keypair via session-scoped vetKey shared between Dashboard and Compose

requirements.summary: Add a session-scoped vetKey context that persists/restores the caller’s transport keypair across navigation and reloads without ever sending the private key to the backend.
acceptanceCriteria: After generating or rotating a transport key on the Dashboard, navigating to the Compose page in the same session uses the existing local transport keypair (no "key not available on this device/tab" error caused by page-level state reset).
The transport private key is never sent to the backend; only the public key is registered as it is today.
The persisted transport keypair can be restored after route changes and full page reloads within the same browser session (sessionStorage-based persistence is acceptable).

file_operations.description: Implement the frontend-only vetKey mechanism: generate/validate a vetKey identifier, store/retrieve the active vetKey for the current session, and provide helpers to build stable sessionStorage keys for the associated transport keypair context.
Add small utilities to safely encode/decode Uint8Array/ArrayBuffer data to/from base64 for sessionStorage persistence of exported WebCrypto keys.

Extend transport key utilities to support exporting/importing the transport private key (and any required metadata) for local-only persistence, enabling restoration into CryptoKey objects after reload; ensure this is strictly browser-local and never involves backend transmission.

Update useTransportKey to accept/use a vetKey context: persist the generated/rotated transport keypair into sessionStorage under the vetKey, restore it on mount/route changes, and keep existing behavior of registering only the public key to the backend; ensure no code path transmits or persists the private key outside the browser.

requirements.summary: Update Dashboard to display the current vetKey and provide copy + navigation/link to Compose including vetKey in the URL so Compose can resolve the correct local transport-key context.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard provides a way to copy the vetKey to clipboard and/or a button that navigates to the Compose route while including the vetKey in the URL.
If the user opens the generated Compose link in a new tab in the same browser session, Compose can still resolve the vetKey and load the transport keypair from session storage.
If no vetKey is available, the Dashboard action is disabled or shows a clear error toast in English.

file_operations.description: Surface the current vetKey in the Dashboard UI (read-only display), add a copy-to-clipboard action, and add a "Go to Compose" (or "Copy Compose Link") action that navigates/builds a URL containing the vetKey query (or hash) parameter; disable the action or show an English toast if vetKey is unavailable.
Optionally extend diagnostics output to include whether a vetKey is present/active and whether a persisted keypair exists for it, improving troubleshooting for the new vetKey-based persistence.

requirements.summary: Update ComposeMessagePage to read vetKey from URL/session and initialize transport-key access using that vetKey so canSend reflects the locally available keypair without requiring regeneration.
acceptanceCriteria: When Compose loads with a valid vetKey in the URL, the page uses it to access the transport keypair and the send eligibility logic ("canSend") can become true without requiring a fresh key generation in Compose.

When Compose loads without a vetKey (or with an invalid vetKey), Compose behaves safely: it does not crash, and it shows the existing diagnostics/error messaging in English indicating the key is unavailable.
The vetKey parameter is handled using the existing URL/session parameter utilities where appropriate (e.g., persisted parameter behavior or hash-secret behavior).

file_operations.description: Read the vetKey from the URL and/or session using existing helpers in frontend/src/utils/urlParams.ts, validate/normalize it via the new vetKey utilities, and initialize transport key access using that vetKey so the existing canSend gating can succeed when the persisted keypair is present; handle missing/invalid vetKey safely with existing English error/diagnostics behavior.

If needed, add a small helper (or extend existing ones) to better support persisted vetKey retrieval across query/hash forms (without changing routing), aligning Compose/Dashboard behavior with the app’s existing persisted-parameter patterns.
